---
title: "activity-level-analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
#| label: Packages 

# Loading needed packages
source("./R/libs.R")

# Read the RDS files containing the data
## Self-reported level of recreational activity
activity_questionnaire_data <- readRDS(file = "data/derivedData/activity_questionnaire_data")

```

### Mean and individual reproducibility of recreational activity level

Kcal/week used on recreational activities

```{r}
#| label: Mean and individual reproducibility of recreational activity level

## Activity 8 weeks before intervention onset
sex_data <- test_data_wide %>% 
  filter(period == "1") %>% 
  filter(timepoint == "pre") %>% 
  select(id, sex)

# Mean (sd) per sex and all participant
activity_before_p1_sex <- activity_before_intervention_questionnaire_data %>% 
  select(id, timepoint, recreational.activities) %>% 
  inner_join(sex_data) %>%
  group_by(sex) %>% 
  summarise(across(recreational.activities,
                   list(mean_sd = ~sprintf("%.0f (%.0f)", mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE))),
                   .names = "{.col}")) %>% print()

activity_before_p1_sex <- activity_before_intervention_questionnaire_data %>% 
  select(id, timepoint, recreational.activities) %>% 
  group_by() %>% 
  summarise(across(recreational.activities,
                   list(mean_sd = ~sprintf("%.0f (%.0f)", mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE))),
                   .names = "{.col}")) %>% print()


## Activity during training intervention

# Selecting variables of interest
activity.p1.washout.p2 <- activity_questionnaire_data %>% 
    select(id, timepoint, recreational.activities) %>% 
  filter((timepoint %in% c("period1", "washout", "period2"))) 


## Comparing recreational activities (kcal/week) in period 1, wash-out, and period 2 using a linear mixed model
model <- lmer(recreational.activities ~ timepoint + (1|id), data = activity.p1.washout.p2) 
coefs <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)
pairs(emmeans(model, specs = "timepoint"))


## Mean (sd) kcal/week for household work, recreational activities, and total activity in ET-1, detraining, and ET-2 to put in text
table.activity <- activity.p1.washout.p2 %>% 
  group_by(timepoint) %>% 
  
  summarise(across(recreational.activities,
                   list(mean_sd = ~sprintf("%.0f (%.0f)", mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE))),
                   .names = "{.col}"))

print(table.activity)



## Reproducibility of individual physical activity level

# Outcomes to analyse
recreational_activities_data <- activity_questionnaire_data %>% 
  select(id, timepoint, recreational.activities) %>% 
  filter(timepoint %in% c("period1", "period2")) %>% 
  mutate(
    period = recode(timepoint,
                    "period1" = "1",
                    "period2" = "2"),
    variable = "recreational.activities",
    value = recreational.activities
  ) %>% 
  select(id, period, variable, value)
  

# Outcomes to analyse
outcomes <- c("recreational.activities") 


## Intraclass correlation

# Function to estimate ICCs with bootstrap CIs
analyze_trainability_icc_ci <- function(
  data,
  outcome,
  nboot = 1000,
  seed  = 123
) {

  message("Running model for: ", outcome)  # Progress message for tracking

  # Subset data to the current outcome and ensure correct factor structure
  df <- data %>%
    filter(variable == outcome) %>%
    mutate(
      period = factor(period),  # Training period as categorical fixed effect
      id     = factor(id)        # Participant ID as grouping factor
    ) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(value))

  # Skip analysis if too few observations are available
  if (nrow(df) < 5) {
    warning("Too few observations for ", outcome)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Fit linear mixed-effects model to change scores
  # Fixed effect: training period (adjusts for systematic differences)
  # Random intercept: participant ID (captures between-individual variability)
  mod <- lmer(
    value ~ 1 + (1 | id),
    data = df,
    REML = TRUE  # REML provides less biased variance component estimates
  )

  # Function to extract ICC from a fitted mixed-effects model
  extract_icc <- function(fit) {
    vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(fit))         # Extract variance components
    vb <- vc$vcov[vc$grp == "id"]              # Between-individual variance
    vw <- vc$vcov[vc$grp == "Residual"]        # Within-individual variance
    vb / (vb + vw)                             # ICC calculation
  }

  # Compute point estimate of the ICC
  ICC_between <- extract_icc(mod)

  
  # Parametric bootstrap to obtain 95% CI for ICC
  boot_res <- tryCatch(
    bootMer(
      mod,
      FUN   = extract_icc,     # Statistic to bootstrap (ICC)
      nsim  = nboot,           # Number of bootstrap replicates
      use.u = TRUE,            # Preserve random-effects structure
      type  = "parametric",    # Parametric bootstrap
      seed  = seed
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL   # Gracefully handle bootstrap failures
  )

  # Extract percentile-based 95% confidence interval
  ci <- if (!is.null(boot_res)) {
    quantile(
      boot_res$t[, 1],
      probs = c(0.025, 0.975),
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  } else {
    c(NA, NA)                  # Return NA if CI cannot be estimated
  }

  # Return tidy summary for the current outcome
  tibble(
    Variable         = outcome,
    ICC_between      = ICC_between,
    ICC_between_low  = ci[1],
    ICC_between_high = ci[2]
  )
}


# Run ICC analysis across all outcomes
results_icc <- map_dfr(
  outcomes,                     # Vector of outcome variable names
  analyze_trainability_icc_ci,  # Analysis function
  data  = recreational_activities_data,       # Long-format dataset
  nboot = 1000                   # Number of bootstrap replicates
)


# Format ICC results for presentation
icc_summary <- results_icc %>%
  mutate(
    `ICC Between` = sprintf(
      "%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]",      # Format ICC with 95% CI
      ICC_between,
      ICC_between_low,
      ICC_between_high
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Variable, `ICC Between`)  # Keep only relevant columns

# Print final table
print(icc_summary, width = Inf)


## Pearsons correlation

r_data <- activity_questionnaire_data %>% 
  select(id, timepoint, recreational.activities) %>% 
  filter(timepoint %in% c("period1", "period2")) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(recreational.activities)) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = timepoint,
              values_from = recreational.activities) %>%
  subset(select = -id) %>%
  print()

# Pearson r
pearsons.r <- r_data %>% 
  {cor.test(.$period1, .$period2, method = "pearson")} %>%
  print()


```
